Treatment Arm(s).value: Standard of care plus abiraterone (abiraterone acetate and prednisolone); Standard of care plus abiraterone and enzalutamide (abiraterone acetate, prednisolone, and enzalutamide)
Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The methods and results sections, as well as Table 1 and Figure 1, clearly describe the experimental arms as 'standard of care plus abiraterone (abiraterone acetate and prednisolone)' in the abiraterone trial, and 'standard of care plus abiraterone and enzalutamide (abiraterone acetate, prednisolone, and enzalutamide)' in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial. These are the names of the experimental arms tested in the clinical trials.

Number of Arms Included.value: 2 in each trial (total 4 arms across both trials)
Number of Arms Included.evidence: The methods and results sections describe two separate randomized controlled trials: the abiraterone trial (standard of care vs. standard of care plus abiraterone) and the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial (standard of care vs. standard of care plus abiraterone plus enzalutamide). Each trial included two arms (one control, one experimental), making a total of four arms across both trials. This is confirmed by the flowchart and Table 1, which list the arms and their patient counts.

Total Participants - N.value: Abiraterone trial: 1003 (502 standard of care, 501 standard of care plus abiraterone); Abiraterone and enzalutamide trial: 916 (454 standard of care, 462 standard of care plus abiraterone and enzalutamide)
Total Participants - N.evidence: The total number of participants for each trial is reported in multiple locations, including the 'Findings' section ('Between Nov 15, 2011, and Jan 17, 2014, 1003 patients were randomly assigned to standard of care (n=502) or standard of care plus abiraterone (n=501) in the abiraterone trial. Between July 29, 2014, and March 31, 2016, 916 patients were randomly assigned to standard of care (n=454) or standard of care plus abiraterone and enzalutamide (n=462) in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial.') and confirmed in Table 1 and Figure 1. These numbers represent all randomized participants included in the efficacy analyses for each arm of both trials.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).value: 96 months (IQR 86–107) in the abiraterone trial and 72 months (61–74) in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial
Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: The median follow-up duration is explicitly reported in the 'Findings' section: 'Median follow-up was 96 months (IQR 86–107) in the abiraterone trial and 72 months (61–74) in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial.' This is confirmed again in the 'Results' section: 'After a median follow-up of 96 months (IQR 86–107), 664 (66%) of 1003 patients died in the abiraterone trial and after a median follow-up of 72 months (61–74), 520 (57%) of 916 died in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial.'

Control Arm - N.evidence: The number of participants in the control arm (standard of care) for each trial is reported in multiple locations, including the 'Findings' section ('1003 patients were randomly assigned to standard of care (n=502) or standard of care plus abiraterone (n=501) in the abiraterone trial. Between July 29, 2014, and March 31, 2016, 916 patients were randomly assigned to standard of care (n=454) or standard of care plus abiraterone and enzalutamide (n=462) in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial.') and in Table 1 and Table 2. Both trials have their own separate control arms: 502 for the abiraterone trial and 454 for the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial.

Control Arm.value: standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy; docetaxel 75 mg/m² intravenously for six cycles with prednisolone 10 mg orally once per day allowed from Dec 17, 2015)
Control Arm.evidence: The control arm is described in the Methods section: 'Patients were randomly assigned (1:1) ... to either standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy; docetaxel 75 mg/m² intravenously for six cycles with prednisolone 10 mg orally once per day allowed from Dec 17, 2015) or standard of care plus abiraterone acetate 1000 mg and prednisolone 5 mg (in the abiraterone trial) ...'. Table 1 and the flowchart also refer to 'standard of care' as the control group. Thus, the control arm is 'standard of care', defined as androgen deprivation therapy, with docetaxel and prednisolone allowed after a protocol amendment.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: Standard of care plus abiraterone (abiraterone acetate 1000 mg and prednisolone 5 mg orally once a day) added to androgen deprivation therapy
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: The Methods section states: 'Patients were randomly assigned (1:1) ... to either standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy; docetaxel 75 mg/m² intravenously for six cycles with prednisolone 10 mg orally once per day allowed from Dec 17, 2015) or standard of care plus abiraterone acetate 1000 mg and prednisolone 5 mg (in the abiraterone trial) orally ...'. The Procedures section confirms: 'Abiraterone acetate (1000 mg) and prednisolone (5 mg; prednisone at Swiss sites) were given orally once a day in the abiraterone trial ...'. Thus, the regimen for Treatment Arm 1 is standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy) plus abiraterone acetate 1000 mg and prednisolone 5 mg orally once daily.

Treatment Arm - N.value: 501 (abiraterone trial: standard of care plus abiraterone), 462 (abiraterone and enzalutamide trial: standard of care plus abiraterone plus enzalutamide)
Treatment Arm - N.evidence: Table 1 and multiple sections in the Results and Methods clearly state that in the abiraterone trial, 501 patients were assigned to the standard of care plus abiraterone arm, and in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial, 462 patients were assigned to the standard of care plus abiraterone plus enzalutamide arm. These are the total numbers of participants included in the experimental arms of each trial.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The Methods section and multiple other locations (e.g., Summary, Figure 1 caption, Table 1) specify that the first experimental arm in the abiraterone trial (Treatment Arm 1) is 'standard of care plus abiraterone acetate and prednisolone.' This is further clarified in the text as 'abiraterone refers to abiraterone acetate and prednisolone.'
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

NCT.evidence: The NCT number is explicitly stated in the Methods section: 'STAMPEDE is registered with ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT00268476) and ISRCTN (ISRCTN78818544)'.

PubMed ID.evidence: The document does not provide a PubMed ID for this publication.

Trial Name.value: Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone with or without enzalutamide for patients with metastatic prostate cancer starting androgen deprivation therapy: final results from two randomised phase 3 trials of the STAMPEDE platform protocol
Trial Name.evidence: The title at the beginning of the document provides the full trial name.

Author.evidence: The author list is provided at the beginning of the document, with Gerhardt Attard listed first, followed by many others, and the phrase 'for the STAMPEDE investigators' indicating a group authorship. 'et al.' is appropriate for brevity.

Year.evidence: The publication year is found in the journal citation: 'Lancet Oncol 2023; 24: 443–56'.

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document is a full publication from The Lancet Oncology, not just an abstract.

Phase.evidence: The Methods section states: 'We analysed two open-label, randomised, controlled, phase 3 trials of the STAMPEDE platform protocol...'

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The document reports the final results from two original phase 3 trials, not a follow-up or secondary analysis.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: A thorough review of the document, including the methods, results, tables, figures, and discussion, shows no mention of quality of life outcomes, assessments, or results. The outcomes reported are overall survival, prostate cancer-specific survival, failure-free survival, progression-free survival, metastatic progression-free survival, symptomatic skeletal-related events, toxic effects/adverse events, and cost-effectiveness. There is no indication that quality of life was measured or reported for any group or subgroup.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: Results are reported by high-volume metastatic disease in Table 1 (baseline characteristics: 'Metastatic volume') and in Figure 2E/F and Figure 3E (overall survival split by high-volume disease), as well as in the text ('outcomes split by low-volume and high-volume disease are of interest and were included as an additional prespecified subgroup analysis').

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: Results are reported by low-volume metastatic disease in Table 1 (baseline characteristics: 'Metastatic volume') and in Figure 2C/D and Figure 3D (overall survival split by low-volume disease), as well as in the text ('outcomes split by low-volume and high-volume disease are of interest and were included as an additional prespecified subgroup analysis').

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.evidence: The document does not report the number or percentage of participants with synchronous metastases (metastases at the time of diagnosis) in the treatment arms (standard of care plus abiraterone or standard of care plus abiraterone and enzalutamide). Baseline characteristics tables and text do not provide a breakdown by mode of metastasis (synchronous vs metachronous).

The document does not report the number or percentage of participants with synchronous metastases in the control arms (standard of care only). Baseline characteristics tables and text do not provide a breakdown by mode of metastasis (synchronous vs metachronous).

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronou.evidence: The document does not report the number or percentage of participants with metachronous metastases (metastases occurring after diagnosis) in the treatment arms. No such breakdown is provided in the tables or text.
The document does not report the number or percentage of participants with metachronous metastases in the control arms. No such breakdown is provided in the tables or text.

Trial.value: Abiraterone trial and Abiraterone and enzalutamide trial of the STAMPEDE platform protocol
Trial.evidence: The document repeatedly refers to two randomised phase 3 trials: the 'abiraterone trial' and the 'abiraterone and enzalutamide trial', both conducted under the STAMPEDE platform protocol. This is explicitly stated in the title ('final results from two randomised phase 3 trials of the STAMPEDE platform protocol') and in the methods section ('two open-label, randomised, controlled, phase 3 trials of the STAMPEDE platform protocol').

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document does not report any measure of quality of life, well-being, or patient-reported outcomes using a standardized questionnaire or survey. The outcomes reported are overall survival, prostate cancer-specific survival, failure-free survival, progression-free survival, metastatic progression-free survival, symptomatic skeletal-related events, toxic effects, adverse events, and cost-effectiveness. There is no mention of a quality of life scale or related results in the main text, tables, or figures.

Primary Endpoint(s).value: Overall survival assessed in the intention-to-treat population (defined as the time from randomisation to death from any cause).
Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: This is explicitly stated in the Methods section under 'The primary outcome was overall survival assessed in the intention-to-treat population.' The definition is further clarified in the Outcomes section: 'The primary outcome was overall survival for both trials (defined as the time from randomisation to death from any cause)'.

This is confirmed in the text: 'Docetaxel was allowed as standard of care for the last 148 (16%) patients randomly assigned in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial.'

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: Prostate cancer-specific survival (time from randomisation to death from prostate cancer); failure-free survival (biochemical failure, local or lymph node progression, distant metastases, or death from prostate cancer); progression-free survival (failure-free survival but excluding biochemical failure); metastatic progression-free survival (progression of or new distant metastases or death from prostate cancer); symptomatic skeletal-related events (pathological fracture or spinal cord compression or bone pain requiring radiotherapy or surgery); toxic effects and adverse events; cost-effectiveness of research treatment.
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: The secondary endpoints are explicitly listed in the Outcomes section: 'Secondary outcomes were prostate cancer-specific survival (defined as the time from randomisation to death from prostate cancer); failure-free survival (defined as biochemical failure, local or lymph node progression, distant metastases, or death from prostate cancer); progression-free survival (defined as failure-free survival but excluding biochemical failure); metastatic progression-free survival (defined as progression of or new distant metastases or death from prostate cancer); symptomatic skeletal-related events (defined as pathological fracture or spinal cord compression or bone pain requiring radiotherapy or surgery); toxic effects and adverse events; and cost-effectiveness of research treatment.'

No. of Deaths - N | Treatment.evidence: From Figure 3A and 3B, the number of deaths in the treatment arms are 293/501 (abiraterone trial, standard of care plus abiraterone) and 228/462 (abiraterone and enzalutamide trial, standard of care plus abiraterone and enzalutamide). Summing these gives the total number of deaths in all treatment arms for the whole population: 293 + 228 = 521.

No. of Deaths - N | Control.evidence: From Figure 3A and 3B, the number of deaths in the control arms are 371/502 (abiraterone trial, standard of care) and 292/454 (abiraterone and enzalutamide trial, standard of care). Summing these gives the total number of deaths in all control arms for the whole population: 371 + 292 = 663.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document describes two large, open-label, randomized, controlled, phase 3 trials with robust methodology: central randomization, intention-to-treat analysis, prespecified statistical analysis plan, and comprehensive reporting of outcomes and adverse events. There is no evidence of selective reporting, attrition bias, or other major methodological flaws. The trials were conducted according to Good Clinical Practice and the Declaration of Helsinki, with independent oversight and data verification. Therefore, the overall risk judgment for the criteria of evidence in these RCTs is 'Low risk of bias'.

Add-on Treatment.value: abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone; abiraterone acetate and prednisolone plus enzalutamide
Add-on Treatment.evidence: The methods and results sections describe two randomized phase 3 trials: one where the add-on treatment was abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (referred to as abiraterone), and another where the add-on treatment was abiraterone acetate and prednisolone plus enzalutamide. Both were given in addition to standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy). This is confirmed in the summary, methods, and throughout the results, including Table 1 and the trial profile figure.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.value: 271 (54%) of 498 (abiraterone trial: standard of care plus abiraterone); 302 (68%) of 445 (abiraterone and enzalutamide trial: standard of care plus abiraterone and enzalutamide)
Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: From the Results section: 'In the first 5 years of treatment, grade 3–5 toxic effects were higher when abiraterone was added to standard of care (271 [54%] of 498 vs 192 [38%] of 502 with standard of care) and the highest toxic effects were seen when abiraterone and enzalutamide were added to standard of care (302 [68%] of 445 vs 204 [45%] of 454 with standard of care).' This refers to all-cause grade 3–5 adverse events in the treatment arms.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.value: 192 (38%) of 502 (abiraterone trial: standard of care); 204 (45%) of 454 (abiraterone and enzalutamide trial: standard of care)
Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: From the Results section: 'In the first 5 years of treatment, grade 3–5 toxic effects were higher when abiraterone was added to standard of care (271 [54%] of 498 vs 192 [38%] of 502 with standard of care) and the highest toxic effects were seen when abiraterone and enzalutamide were added to standard of care (302 [68%] of 445 vs 204 [45%] of 454 with standard of care).' This refers to all-cause grade 3–5 adverse events in the control arms.

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.value: 2 (0.4%) of 445 (abiraterone and enzalutamide trial: standard of care plus abiraterone and enzalutamide)
Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: From the Results section: 'Cardiac causes were the most common cause of death due to adverse events (five [1%] with standard of care plus abiraterone and enzalutamide [two attributed to treatment] and one (<1%) with standard of care in the abiraterone trial; appendix pp 14, 24).' Only the abiraterone and enzalutamide arm specifically reports 2 deaths attributed to treatment (grade 5, treatment-related).

From the Results section: 'Cardiac causes were the most common cause of death due to adverse events (five [1%] with standard of care plus abiraterone and enzalutamide [two attributed to treatment] and one (<1%) with standard of care in the abiraterone trial; appendix pp 14, 24).' This refers to one death in the standard of care arm of the abiraterone trial.

Treatment Class.value: androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) alone; ADT plus abiraterone acetate and prednisolone; ADT plus abiraterone acetate, prednisolone, and enzalutamide
Treatment Class.evidence: The Methods section and Table 1 describe the treatment arms: patients were randomized to standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy), standard of care plus abiraterone acetate and prednisolone, or standard of care plus abiraterone acetate, prednisolone, and enzalutamide. These represent the treatment classes evaluated in the clinical trial.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.value: HR 0.47 (95% CI 0.40–0.55) for abiraterone plus prednisolone; HR 0.50 (95% CI 0.42–0.59) for abiraterone plus prednisolone plus enzalutamide
PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The document reports progression-free survival (PFS) as a secondary outcome. In the Results section and in the summary of secondary endpoints (appendix p 19), the hazard ratio for PFS is given as HR 0.47 (95% CI 0.40–0.55) for the abiraterone trial (treatment: abiraterone plus prednisolone) and HR 0.50 (95% CI 0.42–0.59) for the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial (treatment: abiraterone plus prednisolone plus enzalutamide), both compared to standard of care. No absolute PFS rates (percentages) are reported for the overall population, only hazard ratios.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: No hazard ratio or PFS rate is reported for the control arm alone; all PFS data are presented as hazard ratios comparing treatment to control, not as absolute rates for the control group.

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.value: 48% (43–52) at 84 months (standard of care plus abiraterone); not reached for standard of care plus abiraterone plus enzalutamide
OS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: In the Results section, it states: 'At 84 months in the abiraterone trial, overall survival was 30% (95% CI 26–34) with standard of care versus 48% (43–52) with standard of care plus abiraterone.' For the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial, the survival curve is capped at 84 months and the median OS is reported, but the exact OS rate at 84 months is not directly given. The value for abiraterone plus enzalutamide is not reached (NR) in the subgroup analysis table.

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: In the Results section: 'At 84 months in the abiraterone trial, overall survival was 30% (95% CI 26–34) with standard of care.' This is the only explicit OS rate for the control group at the latest follow-up.

OS Rate (%) | High volume | Treatment.evidence: From Figure 3E (subgroup analysis): For high-volume disease, median OS in the abiraterone trial is 56 months (45–66) for standard of care plus abiraterone, and in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial is 54 months (41–70) for standard of care plus abiraterone plus enzalutamide. The pooled median OS for treatment is 54 months (47–65). No percentage OS rate at a specific time point is given.

The pooled median OS for control is 37 months (34–42). No percentage OS rate at a specific time point is given.

The pooled median OS for treatment is 97 months (92–NR). No percentage OS rate at a specific time point is given.

OS Rate (%) | Low volume | Control.value: 65 months (58–76) median OS (overall, both trials, low-volume, control); 68 months (57–84) abiraterone trial; 64 months (54–76) abiraterone and enzalutamide trial
OS Rate (%) | Low volume | Control.evidence: From Figure 3D (subgroup analysis): For low-volume disease, median OS in the abiraterone trial is 68 months (57–84) for standard of care, and in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial is 64 months (54–76) for standard of care. The pooled median OS for control is 65 months (58–76). No percentage OS rate at a specific time point is given.

Type of Therapy.value: Standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy) alone; standard of care plus abiraterone acetate 1000 mg and prednisolone 5 mg orally once a day; or standard of care plus abiraterone acetate and prednisolone plus enzalutamide 160 mg orally once a day.
Type of Therapy.evidence: The Methods section describes the interventions: patients were randomly assigned to standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy), standard of care plus abiraterone acetate and prednisolone, or standard of care plus abiraterone acetate, prednisolone, and enzalutamide. Dosages and administration routes are specified in the same section.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Table and text (Figure 3A, Overall row) report median overall survival for standard of care plus abiraterone as 77 (68-87) months. This is for the abiraterone trial, which is the experimental arm for the main comparison. The text (Results section) also confirms: 'In the abiraterone trial, median overall survival was 76.6 months (95% CI 67.8–86.9) in the abiraterone group.'

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: Table and text (Figure 3A, Overall row) report median overall survival for standard of care as 46 (42-52) months. The text (Results section) also confirms: 'In the abiraterone trial, median overall survival was ... 45.7 months (41.6–52.0) in the standard of care group.'

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Treatment.evidence: Figure 3E (Overall row) reports median overall survival for high-volume disease in the treatment group (study drug, i.e., abiraterone) as 54 (47-65) months. This is a pooled value from both trials, as per the figure legend and table.

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Control.evidence: Figure 3E (Overall row) reports median overall survival for high-volume disease in the control group as 37 (34-42) months. This is a pooled value from both trials, as per the figure legend and table.

Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Treatment.evidence: Figure 3D (Overall row) reports median overall survival for low-volume disease in the treatment group (study drug, i.e., abiraterone) as 97 (92-NR) months. This is a pooled value from both trials, as per the figure legend and table.

Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Control.evidence: Figure 3D (Overall row) reports median overall survival for low-volume disease in the control group as 65 (58-76) months. This is a pooled value from both trials, as per the figure legend and table.
